setup_code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
device = torch.device('cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu')

tests.name: model_defined
tests.code:
assert hasattr(model, 'forward'), 'model must have a forward method'
assert isinstance(model, nn.Module), 'model must be an nn.Module'
with torch.no_grad():
    test_in = torch.randn(4, 1, 28, 28).to(device)
    out = model(test_in)
    assert out.shape == (4, 10), f'model output shape {out.shape}, expected (4, 10)'

tests.name: model_accuracy
tests.code:
import torchvision, torchvision.transforms as T
import matplotlib
try:
    matplotlib.use('module://matplotlib_inline.backend_inline')
except Exception:
    pass
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
tf = T.Compose([T.ToTensor(), T.Normalize((0.1307,), (0.3081,))])
td = torchvision.datasets.MNIST('./data', train=False, download=True, transform=tf)
loader = torch.utils.data.DataLoader(td, batch_size=1000)
model.eval()
correct = 0; total = 0
all_preds = []; all_labels = []
with torch.no_grad():
    for imgs, labs in loader:
        imgs, labs = imgs.to(device), labs.to(device)
        preds = model(imgs).argmax(1)
        correct += (preds == labs).sum().item()
        total += labs.size(0)
        all_preds.extend(preds.cpu().tolist())
        all_labels.extend(labs.cpu().tolist())
acc = correct/total
print(f'Digit classifier accuracy: {acc:.2%}')

# Show sample predictions
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 8, figsize=(16, 4))
for i in range(16):
    ax = axes[i//8][i%8]
    img = td[i][0].squeeze().numpy()
    ax.imshow(img, cmap='gray')
    pred = all_preds[i]; true = all_labels[i]
    color = 'green' if pred == true else 'red'
    ax.set_title(f'{pred}' + ('' if pred==true else f'({true})'), color=color, fontsize=12)
    ax.axis('off')
plt.suptitle(f'Model Accuracy: {acc:.1%}', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
assert acc >= 0.90, f'Need >=90% accuracy, got {acc:.2%}'

tests.name: training_curves
tests.code:
import matplotlib
try:
    matplotlib.use('module://matplotlib_inline.backend_inline')
except Exception:
    pass
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
assert 'train_losses' in dir() or 'train_losses' in globals(), 'train_losses list not found'
assert len(train_losses) >= 2, f'Need at least 2 epochs of training, got {len(train_losses)}'
assert train_losses[-1] < train_losses[0], 'Loss should decrease during training'
assert 'test_accs' in dir() or 'test_accs' in globals(), 'test_accs list not found'
assert len(test_accs) >= 2
print(f'Training: {len(train_losses)} epochs, final loss={train_losses[-1]:.4f}, final acc={test_accs[-1]:.2%}')

# Plot training curves
fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
ax1.plot(train_losses, 'b-', linewidth=1.5)
ax1.set_xlabel('Epoch'); ax1.set_ylabel('Loss'); ax1.set_title('Training Loss', fontsize=14)
ax1.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
ax2.plot(test_accs, 'r-', linewidth=1.5)
ax2.set_xlabel('Epoch'); ax2.set_ylabel('Accuracy'); ax2.set_title('Test Accuracy', fontsize=14)
ax2.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.suptitle('Training Curves', fontsize=16)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

tests.name: predict_sum_defined
tests.code:
# Check predict_sum runs and returns correct shape
import torchvision, torchvision.transforms as T
tf = T.Compose([T.ToTensor(), T.Normalize((0.1307,), (0.3081,))])
td = torchvision.datasets.MNIST('./data', train=False, download=True, transform=tf)
img1 = td[0][0].unsqueeze(0).to(device)
img2 = td[1][0].unsqueeze(0).to(device)
with torch.no_grad():
    result = predict_sum(model, img1, img2)
assert result is not None, 'predict_sum returned None'
result_t = torch.tensor(result) if not isinstance(result, torch.Tensor) else result
assert result_t.numel() == 1, f'Expected 1 prediction, got {result_t.numel()}'
val = result_t.item()
assert 0 <= val <= 18, f'Sum prediction {val} out of range [0, 18]'

tests.name: predict_sum_batch
tests.code:
# Test batched prediction
import torchvision, torchvision.transforms as T
tf = T.Compose([T.ToTensor(), T.Normalize((0.1307,), (0.3081,))])
td = torchvision.datasets.MNIST('./data', train=False, download=True, transform=tf)
imgs = torch.stack([td[i][0] for i in range(16)]).to(device)
img1, img2 = imgs[:8], imgs[8:]
with torch.no_grad():
    result = predict_sum(model, img1, img2)
result_t = torch.tensor(result) if not isinstance(result, torch.Tensor) else result
assert result_t.shape[0] == 8, f'Batch of 8 should give 8 predictions, got {result_t.shape}'
assert (result_t >= 0).all() and (result_t <= 18).all(), 'Predictions out of range'

tests.name: addition_accuracy
tests.code:
# Addition accuracy test
import torchvision, torchvision.transforms as T
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
tf = T.Compose([T.ToTensor(), T.Normalize((0.1307,), (0.3081,))])
td = torchvision.datasets.MNIST('./data', train=False, download=True, transform=tf)

class AddDS(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, mnist, n=5000, seed=99):
        self.mnist = mnist
        rng = __import__('numpy').random.RandomState(seed)
        self.pairs = rng.randint(0, len(mnist), (n, 2))
    def __len__(self): return len(self.pairs)
    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        i, j = self.pairs[idx]
        img1, l1 = self.mnist[i]; img2, l2 = self.mnist[j]
        return img1, img2, l1 + l2

add_ds = AddDS(td, n=5000, seed=99)
add_loader = DataLoader(add_ds, batch_size=256)
model.eval()
correct = 0; total = 0
with torch.no_grad():
    for i1, i2, tgt in add_loader:
        i1, i2 = i1.to(device), i2.to(device)
        p = predict_sum(model, i1, i2)
        p_t = torch.tensor(p).cpu() if not isinstance(p, torch.Tensor) else p.cpu()
        correct += (p_t == tgt).sum().item()
        total += tgt.size(0)
acc = correct/total
print(f'Addition accuracy: {acc:.2%}')
assert acc >= 0.70, f'Need >=70% addition accuracy, got {acc:.2%}'

tests.name: model_accuracy_bonus
tests.code:
import torchvision, torchvision.transforms as T
tf = T.Compose([T.ToTensor(), T.Normalize((0.1307,), (0.3081,))])
td = torchvision.datasets.MNIST('./data', train=False, download=True, transform=tf)
loader = torch.utils.data.DataLoader(td, batch_size=1000)
model.eval()
correct = 0; total = 0
with torch.no_grad():
    for imgs, labs in loader:
        imgs, labs = imgs.to(device), labs.to(device)
        preds = model(imgs).argmax(1)
        correct += (preds == labs).sum().item()
        total += labs.size(0)
acc = correct/total
assert acc >= 0.99, f'Bonus: need >=99%, got {acc:.2%}'
print(f'BONUS: Digit accuracy {acc:.2%} exceeds 99%!')

tests.name: addition_accuracy_bonus
tests.code:
# Bonus: high addition accuracy
import torchvision, torchvision.transforms as T
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
tf = T.Compose([T.ToTensor(), T.Normalize((0.1307,), (0.3081,))])
td = torchvision.datasets.MNIST('./data', train=False, download=True, transform=tf)

class AddDS(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, mnist, n=5000, seed=99):
        self.mnist = mnist
        rng = __import__('numpy').random.RandomState(seed)
        self.pairs = rng.randint(0, len(mnist), (n, 2))
    def __len__(self): return len(self.pairs)
    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        i, j = self.pairs[idx]
        img1, l1 = self.mnist[i]; img2, l2 = self.mnist[j]
        return img1, img2, l1 + l2

add_ds = AddDS(td, n=5000, seed=99)
add_loader = DataLoader(add_ds, batch_size=256)
model.eval()
correct = 0; total = 0
with torch.no_grad():
    for i1, i2, tgt in add_loader:
        i1, i2 = i1.to(device), i2.to(device)
        p = predict_sum(model, i1, i2)
        p_t = torch.tensor(p).cpu() if not isinstance(p, torch.Tensor) else p.cpu()
        correct += (p_t == tgt).sum().item()
        total += tgt.size(0)
acc = correct/total
assert acc >= 0.95, f'Bonus: need >=95% addition accuracy, got {acc:.2%}'
print(f'BONUS: Addition accuracy {acc:.2%} exceeds 95%!')

tests.name: bonus_convolution
tests.code:
# Bonus: check if student uses probability convolution
import inspect
try:
    src = inspect.getsource(predict_sum)
except:
    src = ''
uses_conv = any(kw in src.lower() for kw in ['conv', 'convolve', 'np.convolve', 'correlate', 'fft', 'polynomial', 'poly'])
# Also check: does it use softmax/probabilities?
uses_probs = any(kw in src.lower() for kw in ['softmax', 'prob', 'exp(', 'F.softmax'])
if uses_conv or uses_probs:
    print('BONUS: Student appears to use probability-based approach!')
else:
    print('No probability convolution detected (checking code pattern only).')
    assert False, 'Bonus not awarded — no probability convolution pattern detected'
